qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default AUTO_LOGIN exposes unauthenticated superuser session issuance
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is rooted in default AUTO_LOGIN behavior, startup superuser bootstrap, and unauthenticated token minting in login/auth code, not the missing get_current_active_user dependency on the advisory's monitor.py endpoints.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Global MCP resource listing exposes cross-user V1 file download URLs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding depends on an unscoped MCP flow query and unauthenticated file download routes in mcp_utils.py and files.py, not the advisory's missing authentication dependency on monitor.py message and transaction endpoints.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the session's qualifying findings identify the advisory-described root cause: missing get_current_active_user authentication dependencies on three /api/v1/monitor/* endpoints in src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/monitor.py.
evidence: The advisory is specific to GET /api/v1/monitor/messages, GET /api/v1/monitor/transactions, and DELETE /api/v1/monitor/messages/session/{session_id} in src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/monitor.py.
auto_login_default_superuser_takeover instead cites src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/login.py, auth settings defaults, and startup bootstrap logic for unauthenticated superuser token issuance.

mcp_resource_listing_cross_user_file_read instead cites src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/mcp_utils.py and src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/files.py for cross-user file URL disclosure and unauthenticated download access.
